title: Use of eval() in Python
description: The use of eval() on arbitrary input allows for arbitrary code execution, which is a major security vulnerability.
reasoning: The eval() function interprets a string as Python code. If the input source is untrusted, an attacker can execute arbitrary commands on the host machine, leading to complete system compromise.
suggested_fix: Use a safe alternative like ast.literal_eval() if you only need to evaluate literals, or implement a whitelist of allowed commands/functions.
